text.title: Agents Framework Selection — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure from input brief to signed-off output decision-record.

list.item: Input: Use case description.
Action: Document task shape (single-agent vs multi-agent), tool count, loop depth, deployment target.
Output: scope.md with these 4 fields populated.
Decision gate: If scope is fuzzy, sharpen it before picking a framework.
Input: scope.md.
Action: Score (graph-vs-role, observability, deployment) for each candidate framework.
Output: scoring table with rows = frameworks, cols = axes.
Decision gate: If two frameworks tie, pick the one with the larger community.
Input: Scoring table.
Action: Pick top scorer; list ≥2 rejected alternatives with one-sentence reason.
Output: Decision record draft with rejected section.
Decision gate: If rejections feel forced, the scoring may be biased — redo step 2.
Input: Tech lead name.
Action: Set 6-month review date + named owner.
Output: Decision record finalised.
Decision gate: If no named owner exists, escalate before finalising.
Input: Decision record.
Action: Build a 1-day spike with the chosen framework; confirm it fits.
Output: Spike report + go/no-go to commit.
Decision gate: If spike fails, return to step 2 with new data.
